reason: Closed-book solvability failure: option A is labeled Correct but is not derivable from the stem plus general engineering reasoning. The stem only states NCA vent gas has higher laminar burning velocity than typical hydrocarbons; it does not specify any 'standard hydrocarbon-based explosion pressure model' applicability domain or how peak pressure scales with laminar burning velocity in confined geometries. Thus A requires unstated, paper-specific/model-specific assumptions (or additional parameters like turbulence, congestion, confinement, composition) and is not uniquely admissible. Options C and E are solvable, but a single non-solvable Correct option makes the item inadmissible.

MCQ with papers provided. The stem contains all needed numerical values and observations (peak pressures at specified equivalence ratios; oscillations not reproduced). Incorrect options are not plausibly defensible under the stated conditions without adding extra assumptions, so the item is closed-book solvable and non-ambiguous.

Short-answer item; papers provided. The stem contains all necessary facts (LBV range, pressure range tied to partly confined geometry and varied release profiles, and CFD peak-pressure agreement with failure on oscillations). The gold answer’s limitations and consequences follow from general engineering reasoning about geometry-specific explosion data and model-validation scope, and none of its required claims depend on paper-only definitions or extra numerical thresholds.
